commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Telegram explicit bot-handle mention metadata to inbound context, adds Telegram-only assistant identity prompt wording/docs/tests, and adjusts Docker E2E heartbeat process cleanup.

PR surface: Source +78, Tests +65, Docs +4, Other +12. Total +159 across 11 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: source inspection shows resolveInboundMentionDecision can admit reply_to_bot implicit turns with raw wasMentioned=false and effectiveWasMentioned=true, while this PR passes the raw value into ctx.WasMentioned. I did not run live Telegram in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can fix the mention projection bug and add regression coverage; maintainer judgment remains for accepting the scoped Telegram policy and the Docker helper scope.

target.title: fix(telegram): bind bot mentions to assistant identity
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes Telegram group/topic turns where an explicit @bot-handle mention could still be interpreted as addressing someone other than the current assistant, allowing a run to execute tools and then end as `NO_REPLY`. The patch carries explicit bot mention metadata into shared inbound context and adds a Telegram-only prompt line binding the configured bot handle to the assistant identity.

This PR is intentionally scoped to the bot-handle identity slice. It does not claim to close the broader linked Telegram reply-loss cluster.

## Linked context

Related openclaw/openclaw#85654. Live before-fix repro evidence: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/85654#issuecomment-4703305519.

## Real behavior proof

- Issue addressed: Telegram explicit bot-handle mention in a forum-topic group routes as an assistant-addressed turn and produces a visible reply instead of `NO_REPLY`.
- Environment tested: PR branch `b819faf00ebaa65b6e0b6ef8b92aabedd4119c40` running as a local staging OpenClaw gateway on port `18794`, isolated state root `~/.local/state/openclaw-staging/pr93088-proof`, Telegram staging bot `@kesslerClawBot`, staging supergroup `-1003908474243`, topic `1`.
- Exact steps or command run after fix: Started the PR branch gateway with `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=~/.local/state/openclaw-staging/pr93088-proof OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=~/.local/state/openclaw-staging/pr93088-proof/openclaw.json node openclaw.mjs --log-level debug gateway --port 18794 --bind loopback --allow-unconfigured --verbose`, then sent `@kesslerClawBot reply exactly: PR93088_VISIBLE_REPLY_OK` in the staging Telegram group topic.
- Evidence: Redacted runtime log and copied live session output from the staging OpenClaw run:

```text
Redacted gateway log excerpt:
20:51:38 [telegram] update: {"update_id":471279866,"message":{"message_id":22,"chat":{"is_forum":true,"type":"supergroup"},"text":"[redacted]","entities":[{"offset":0,"length":15,"type":"mention"}]}}

Redacted trajectory/session evidence:
sessionKey: agent:niemand:telegram:group:-1003908474243:topic:1
chat_id: telegram:-1003908474243
topic_id: 1
is_forum: true
is_group_chat: true
was_mentioned: true
explicitly_mentioned_bot: true
mention_source: explicit_bot
user: @kesslerClawBot reply exactly: PR93088_VISIBLE_REPLY_OK
assistant model output: PR93088_VISIBLE_REPLY_OK
OpenClaw delivery mirror: 🤖 PR93088_VISIBLE_REPLY_OK
NO_REPLY final answer present for this turn: false
```

- Observed result: The PR branch produced a visible Telegram group/topic reply for an explicit bot-handle request: `🤖 PR93088_VISIBLE_REPLY_OK`. The session artifact contains no `NO_REPLY` final answer for this turn.
- Not tested: This proof covers only the bot-handle identity slice in this PR. It does not claim the broader Telegram reply-loss cluster is fully fixed.
- Proof limitations environment constraints: Proof used local staging Telegram and redacted runtime logs/session artifacts rather than Mantis-owned Telegram Desktop recording, because Mantis issue-comment proof requires repository write/maintain/admin permission.
- Before evidence optional but encouraged: Live before-fix production repro is linked above in openclaw/openclaw#85654.

## Tests validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/channels/inbound-event/context.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/groups.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:prod`
- `pnpm docs:list`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` -> `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`
- `gh pr checks 93088 --repo openclaw/openclaw --json name,state,workflow,link` -> 165 checks, `bad: []`

## Risk checklist

- User-visible behavior changed: Yes, only Telegram prompts and context metadata for explicit bot-handle mentions.
- Config, environment, migration behavior changed: No.
- Security, auth, secrets, network, tool execution behavior changed: No.
- Highest-risk area: prompt wording in group chats. Mitigation: Telegram-gated prompt text, structured mention facts, retained `NO_REPLY` policy, tests for explicit bot mentions versus non-Telegram behavior, and staging Telegram group/topic proof.

## Current review state

Local validation is ready and redacted staging Telegram group/topic proof is now supplied. Broader Telegram reply-loss remains out of scope.

---

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
